questions.main_text: Millä kahdella tavalla voi houkutella puutarhaansa hyödyllisiä eläimiä?
questions.feedback:
– säilyttämällä vanhat tiilirakenteet (2 p.)

– jättämällä pihalle lehtikasoja (2 p.)

content.text: Millä kahdella tavalla voi houkutella puutarhaansa hyödyllisiä eläimiä?

questions.main_text: Mitä Jennifer Connell yritti tehdä oikeudessa? Mikä oli virallinen lopputulos asiassa?
questions.feedback:
– hän yritti saada korvauksia haastamalla veljenpoikansa oikeuteen (2 p.)

– oikeus hylkäsi kanteen (2 p.)

content.text: Mitä Jennifer Connell yritti tehdä oikeudessa? Mikä oli virallinen lopputulos asiassa?

questions.main_text: Mikä metsästäjää ihmetytti? Mikä todennäköisesti aiheutti ilmiön?
questions.feedback:
– maanvyörymä (2 p.)

− pohjavesi / maanalainen vesi (2 p.)

content.text: Mikä metsästäjää ihmetytti? Mikä todennäköisesti aiheutti ilmiön?

questions.main_text: Miksi sairaaloissa voi tulla puutetta lastenlääkäreistä? (2 asiaa)
questions.feedback:
– he voivat jäädä äitiyslomille / hankkia lapsia (2 p.)

– he haluavat tehdä lyhyttä työpäivää / osa-aikatyötä (2 p.)

content.text: Miksi sairaaloissa voi tulla puutetta lastenlääkäreistä? (2 asiaa)

questions.main_text: Mitä Martin Sholl näki järven pohjassa? Miksi hän poisti ne?
questions.feedback:
– muoviluurangot pöydän ääressä (2 p.)

− media ei ottanut häntä huomioon (2 p.)

content.text: Mitä Martin Sholl näki järven pohjassa? Miksi hän poisti ne?

VI Tässä osassa kuulet viisi uutista. Kuulet kunkin uutisen vain kerran. Kuultuasi uutisen vastaa kysymykseen **lyhyesti suomeksi**. Kirjoita vastauksesi **selvällä käsialalla** kielikokeen vastauslomakkeen **A-puolelle**.

questions.main_text: What is this message talking about?

content.text: What is this message talking about?

options.text: The many unanswered questions we face daily
The unnoticed people who keep things running
The failure of the authorities to uphold order

questions.main_text:
[CLIP id=1-4333789]

V You will hear four radio soundbites. You will hear each once. Answer questions 23–26.

content.text:
[CLIP id=1-4333789]

V You will hear four radio soundbites. You will hear each once. Answer questions 23–26.

questions.main_text: What do the two think about the fan’s motivation?

content.text: What do the two think about the fan’s motivation?

options.text: He was manipulated by the TV crew
He wanted a moment of fame
He felt the fast food was enticing

questions.main_text: What do the two think about the situation?

content.text: What do the two think about the situation?

options.text: We are overexposed to grossness
They find the animal quite resourceful
The video picture is poor quality

questions.main_text: Why is fizzy pop not permitted in the competition?

content.text: Why is fizzy pop not permitted in the competition?

options.text: It harms the participants’ teeth
It raises energy levels too much
It brings in unwanted commercialisation

questions.main_text: Why were the sheep on the streets?

content.text: Why were the sheep on the streets?

options.text: To keep up an economic necessity
To sustain an urban way of life
To preserve domestic production

questions.main_text: Why was Marx’s grave moved?

content.text: Why was Marx’s grave moved?

options.text: To improve traffic flow and visitor security
To satisfy the customers and authorities
To increase its accessibility and visibility

questions.main_text: What do some people do at the cemetery?

content.text: What do some people do at the cemetery?

options.text: Try to steal a glance at Marx’s grave
Organise a protest against commercialism
Find alternative routes in

questions.main_text: What do the Marxists think about the fee?

content.text: What do the Marxists think about the fee?

options.text: It is annoying
It is innovative
It is unbiased

questions.main_text: Why is this arrangement suitable for Jamal?

content.text: Why is this arrangement suitable for Jamal?

options.text: It supplies his loan payments
It gives him alternative income
It means he needs to keep his car

questions.main_text: Who want to place advertisements on Jamal Afridi’s car?

content.text: Who want to place advertisements on Jamal Afridi’s car?

options.text: Small, local businesses
Various advertising corporations
A broad range of firms

questions.main_text: How does Jamal manage his personal finances?

content.text: How does Jamal manage his personal finances?

options.text: He is economical indeed
He is saving most of his wages
He is rather disorganised

questions.main_text: What situation does the rise of house prices cause?

content.text: What situation does the rise of house prices cause?

options.text: People’s bank accounts have comparatively less value
The construction business is flourishing
People are losing their jobs

questions.main_text: How does Moshe get to work?

content.text: How does Moshe get to work?

options.text: His workmates pick him up
His job includes driver training
His family members take turns helping out

questions.main_text: What doesn’t Moshe like to do?

content.text: What doesn’t Moshe like to do?

options.text: Work overly long hours at academic subjects
Continually emphasize differences between people
Solely rely on inspiration to start studying

questions.main_text: What are we asked to think about?

content.text: What are we asked to think about?

options.text: The impact of the media in the world
The reliability of our sources of information
The role of the UN in conflict resolution

questions.main_text: What is said about planning for winter?

content.text: What is said about planning for winter?

options.text: Our approach affects how we cope
We must have plenty of heavy coats
We need bright light devices

questions.main_text: What type of people are viewed as self-confident?

content.text: What type of people are viewed as self-confident?

options.text: Those who have a crowd of people around
Those moving in small circles
Those from both categories mentioned

questions.main_text: What does the woman say about women’s roles?

content.text: What does the woman say about women’s roles?

options.text: They haven’t changed enough in the last generation
Too much stress is placed on non-essentials
Basic feelings are not considered

questions.main_text: What probably caused the accident?

content.text: What probably caused the accident?

options.text: A mechanical failure
A pothole in the road
A human miscalculation

content.text: How do Friends of Highgate Cemetery defend their practice?

options.text: It keeps the crowds of tourists manageable
It meets operating expenses
It pays for their guides and ticket sellers

questions.main_text: Why does Jamal Afridi need to do extra work?

content.text: Why does Jamal Afridi need to do extra work?

options.text: He has a poor salary
He is establishing a family
He has an unsuitable job

questions.main_text: What is a problem for young homebuyers?

content.text: What is a problem for young homebuyers?

options.text: They have no savings
They don’t know how to get a bank loan
They can’t do extra hours of work

questions.main_text: Why was Moshe chosen for this job at NASA?

content.text: Why was Moshe chosen for this job at NASA?

options.text: His qualifications were the best
His mentors guided him in this direction
His skill set matched the job description

questions.main_text: What balances out studying for Moshe?

content.text: What balances out studying for Moshe?

options.text: Regular care for the disadvantaged
Everyday, ordinary activities
Various extracurricular accomplishments

questions.main_text: Why isn’t Moshe in university right now?

content.text: Why isn’t Moshe in university right now?

options.text: He is waiting to receive another scholarship
He has found an intriguing task
He had earlier planned it that way

questions.main_text: Why did Moshe Cavalin start college?

content.text: Why did Moshe Cavalin start college?

options.text: His parents could no longer teach him
His mother wanted him to get a public education
His mentors wanted to see how far he could go
